title: Factory Intent
description: A high-level spec or intent that a planner decomposes into a feature and dev/qa packet pairs. The spec body may be provided inline via 'spec' (small intents) or by reference via 'spec_path' (large human-authored Markdown specs). Exactly one of the two must be present.

id.description: Kebab-case unique identifier. Must match the filename (without .json).

title.description: Human-readable summary of the requested outcome.

spec.description: Inline high-level problem statement or desired outcome for the planner to decompose. Use for short intents. Mutually exclusive with 'spec_path'.

spec_path.description: Path (relative to the project root) to a Markdown file containing the authoritative spec body. Use for large, human-authored specs that already live alongside the code. Must not be absolute and must not escape the project root. Mutually exclusive with 'spec'.

constraints.description: Optional constraints or requirements the planner must honor.

depends_on.description: Spec-level dependencies copied from the source spec's frontmatter. Each entry is the kebab-case id of another spec/intent that must complete before this one runs. Optional and additive (Phase 4 of single-entry-pipeline); existing intents without this field continue to validate.

status.description: Intent lifecycle status. 'approved' grants planner and supervisor authority to execute derived artifacts within scope. Planning produces a feature; execution remains on the feature and packets.

feature_id.description: Linked feature artifact generated from this intent.

planned_at.description: When planning completed and the linked feature/packets were created.

approved_at.description: When the human approved the intent/spec for planner decomposition and downstream execution.
